channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Security guard apologises for inappropriate comments during SAPS caravan incident
item.description: A security officer employed by a private security company under a joint law enforcement partnership with SAPS has apologised for making inappropriate remarks during a viral video filmed inside a polic...

item.title: Sonam Wangchuk Admitted to Hospital After Fall
item.description: Sonam Wangchuk, an Indian environmentalist and former Jammu and Kashmir Lieutenant Governor, has been hospitalized following a fall. The incident occurred while he was in a remote area, and his condit...

item.title: WNBA Suspends Coach Brondello for Inappropriate Comment on Angel Reese
item.description: WNBA coach Sandy Brondello has been suspended for one game without pay after making an 'inappropriate' comment about Atlanta star Angel Reese following a game. The incident occurred during a match whe...

item.title: Chinar Book Festival promotes literacy and cultural exchange in Kashmir
item.description: The third edition of the Chinar Book Festival, held in Srinagar, attracted thousands of attendees despite summer heat, highlighting its significance as Jammu and Kashmir's largest literary event. Inau...

item.title: China's Growing Influence in Global Governance: An Outsider's View
item.description: The article explores China's significant role in shaping global governance, highlighting its transformation from a struggling economy to a global power. It discusses the ECOWAS Headquarters project as...

item.title: U.S. Marshals Arrest Tate Brothers in Miami on UK Extradition Request for Rape and Sex Trafficking Charges
item.description: Andrew and Tristan Tate, social media influencers with dual American and British citizenship, were arrested by U.S. Marshals in Miami on July 18, 2026, following a UK extradition request. The UK Crown...

item.title: Cayetanos Address Impeachment Concerns via Facebook Live
item.description: The article discusses the Cayetanos' decision to address impeachment-related issues through a Facebook Live session, highlighting their efforts to engage the public on matters of political accountabil...

item.title: ICE Agent With History of Domestic Abuse Killed During Traffic Stop
item.description: A Washington Post report detailed the violent history of David Brouillette, an ICE agent who fatally shot Colombian man Joan Sebastian Guerrero during a traffic stop in Maine. The incident follows all...

item.title: Eurovision Contest Winner Announced: A New Era Begins
item.description: The Eurovision Song Contest, one of the most iconic and long-standing international music competitions, has concluded with a decisive victory for the song 'Dancing Queen' performed by the Swedish arti...

item.title: Legal aid initiatives between Tanzania and Germany support millions
item.description: The collaboration between Tanzania and Germany in providing legal aid has significantly impacted the lives of over five million Tanzanians. This initiative, launched to address legal challenges and pr...

item.title: Montreal Alouettes edge Calgary Stampeders 38-32 with record-breaking performance
item.description: The Montreal Alouettes secured a thrilling 38-32 victory over the Calgary Stampeders in a CFL matchup, with quarterback Davis Alexander setting a league record for consecutive passes without an interc...

item.title: Luxurious Skoda Superb Offers Premium Features at Entry-Level SUV Price
item.description: The article highlights a used 2018 Skoda Superb L&K sedan available for Rs. 12 lakh, slightly cheaper than the top-spec Tata Punch. This luxury sedan offers a 1.8-liter turbo petrol engine producing 1...

item.title: Tulsi Gabbard's Brother Jailed for Attempting to Lure Children to Hotel Room
item.description: Batarti Gabbard, the 55-year-old brother of former Director of National Intelligence Tulsi Gabbard, was arrested for allegedly trying to lure children to a hotel room in Honolulu, Hawaii. According to...

item.title: Teenager faces legal action after speeding 70kmh over parkway limit
item.description: A 17-year-old P-plater has had their licence suspended and vehicle seized after being caught speeding at 170kmh on the Tuggeranong Parkway, 70kmh over the posted limit. Police stopped the driver follo...

item.title: Tiger Beer Launches Lighter Version to Attract Younger Audience
item.description: Tiger Beer, a popular Nigerian brand, has relaunched its product with a lighter, smoother taste and refreshed packaging to attract younger consumers. The new version, called Tiger 4.0, features a refo...

item.title: Iran's Supreme Leader Condemns US Breaches of Ceasefire Agreement
item.description: Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei has strongly criticized the United States for repeatedly breaching a ceasefire agreement, calling President Donald Trump's signature on the deal 'utterly w...

item.title: Fish Hoek Surf Lifesaving Club Marks 70 Years of Service and Success
item.description: The Fish Hoek Surf Lifesaving Club, founded in 1958 by seven Sea Scouts including Clive Duffell, has grown from humble beginnings to become one of South Africa's most successful surf lifesaving organi...

item.title: Iran and US Escalate Gulf Conflict with Attacks and Casualties
item.description: Iran has intensified attacks on US military targets in the Gulf region, resulting in the deaths of two American personnel and one missing in action. The conflict follows the collapse of an interim cea...

item.title: US-Iran conflict escalates with military strikes and diplomatic tensions
